headline: How Investors Are Reacting To Applied Materials (AMAT) Being Dropped From Key Russell Value Indices
summary: In late June 2026, Applied Materials, Inc. was removed from several Russell value-oriented indices, including the Russell 1000 Value-Defensive and Russell 3000 Value benchmarks. This index reshuffling comes as Applied Materials is increasingly tied to AI-driven semiconductor equipment demand and advanced packaging, shifting how some investors may categorize the stock. Next, we’ll examine how stronger multi-year visibility into AI-related equipment demand could reshape Applied Materials’...

headline: Chips Made In The USA Are Making A Comeback. What Investors Should Know.
summary: Semiconductor manufacturing was dominated by American companies before factories moved to countries like Taiwan and South Korea.  Now, hints of that original vigor are emerging again as a fab construction boom promises to revive what once was the global standard: computer chips made in the USA.  "Today, all of the major semiconductor manufacturers capable of building a state-of-the-art semiconductor are investing in the United States," said Joe Stockunas, president of the Americas unit of the SEMI industry trade group.

raw.headline: Chips Made In The USA Are Making A Comeback. What Investors Should Know.
